close_reason: Added PostCommands field to WrapperRule. Go wrapper now defaults to running 'go mod tidy' after go get/mod commands to ensure correct direct/indirect classification.
description:
## Problem

When adding new Go imports, the go.mod file may be updated with dependencies marked as `// indirect` even when they should be direct. This causes issues with the Nix build because the generated vendor/modules.txt doesn't mark them as `## explicit`.

Example: Adding `go.starlark.net/syntax` import caused build failures because starlark was marked indirect in go.mod.

## Solution

The `tw` wrapper (or a pre-commit hook) should run `go mod tidy` automatically after any command that modifies go.mod. This ensures:
- Direct vs indirect dependencies are correctly classified
- Unused dependencies are removed
- go-deps.toml stays in sync (via the existing sync mechanism)

## Implementation options

1. **Extend tw wrapper**: After running `go get`, `go mod download`, etc., automatically run `go mod tidy` if go.mod changed
2. **Pre-commit hook**: Run `go mod tidy` as part of the pre-commit checks
3. **Both**: tw for immediate feedback, pre-commit as safety net

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `go mod tidy` runs automatically when go.mod is modified
- [ ] Direct imports are never marked as indirect after the auto-tidy
- [ ] go-deps.toml is regenerated with correct direct/indirect flags
title: Run go mod tidy automatically after go.mod changes
